Chris Smallfield

Scriptyard

Scriptyard

Maker

📌 [...] Hi everyone, I’m Chris, an Emmy-nominated VFX Supervisor, and I’ve spent more than 20 years working in film and television. Over that time I’ve seen countless writers and filmmakers develop stories using walls covered in index cards. It’s a wonderfully simple way to think about structure, but it’s not always practical once you’re working remotely or collaborating with other people. I built Scriptyard because I wanted that same experience in a browser. It’s a virtual notecard workspace for developing stories visually. You can organise scenes, move ideas around, explore different structures and keep track of the bigger picture without fighting a complicated interface. I also wanted it to be free. One thing I’ve noticed over the last couple of years is that many writers are understandably [...] Report

@roopesh\_donde Since this isn’t a project that’s monetized at all (nor ever will be) I wanted to wait and see how much of an issue this was for people. I think I might make something like a Google Drive integration so people can share access through their own space and still not need to share anything with my server. Thanks for taking the time try it and write to me!

results.content:
Scriptyard

# Scriptyard

A free visual story development workspace

## About Scriptyard [...] Scriptyard is an innovative visual story development workspace designed specifically for screenwriters, filmmakers, and storytellers. Its unique infinite canvas allows users to map out characters, locations, scenes, and story structures in a spatial, intuitive manner, making complex story planning more manageable and visually engaging. The platform supports exporting projects to popular script formats like Markdown, Fountain, and Final Draft, facilitating seamless integration into production workflows. What sets Scriptyard apart is its focus on spatial storytelling—providing a flexible, visual environment that enhances creativity and organization, especially for those working on intricate narratives or multi-threaded plots. Whether you're developing a screenplay, planning a YouTube [...] Whether you're developing a screenplay, planning a YouTube series, or plotting a movie, Scriptyard offers a dynamic workspace that encourages experimentation and clarity.

results.title:
Hello all!

I’ve just launched a new tool for story development, Scriptyard! I’m quite proud of it and find it to be pretty fun to use, I’m a bit biased of course. You can try it now for free, I’d… | Christopher Smallfield | 11 comments
results.content:
Details Below :
Scriptyard is a free, browser-based story-planning tool made by independent studio Minimal Humans. It gives screenwriters and storytellers an infinite canvas for thinking spatially — arranging cards and backdrops to map characters, locations, scenes, and overall structure before a word of script is written. When the plan is ready, a single click exports it to Markdown, Fountain, or Final Draft, so writers can move straight into drafting in the tool they already use. Scriptyard is private by default: nothing is stored on a server, work stays in the writer's own browser, and there is no login, subscription, or ads. It is designed for desktop and tablet and also works on mobile.
About Minimal Humans: [...] About Minimal Humans:
Minimal Humans is a two-person creative studio that builds elegant, no-friction tools and shares them openly. The team makes the software it wishes existed and releases it for others to use. Elegant solutions for Everyone. Alongside Scriptyard, the studio develops [Rivett, a fast Rust-based image viewer]. The studio is based in Berlin. [...] So now when you’re reviewing what the agent did for you, or looking at a pull request of somebody else’s code, you can see a visual recap instead of just reading a wall of text.
It’s all free and open source. You can find it on my GitHub.
Will link to it in the reply because we all know how dumb these algorithms are with links.
